file_title: Enhancing Patient Communication for Service Efficiency
introduction: As we continue to grow in the virtual healthcare space, improving patient communication is paramount. This presentation will explore strategies to optimize our communication channels and reduce service calls.

subsections.heading: Current State of Customer Service Interaction
subsections.content:
• In Q3 2024, our corporate program participants benefitted from pre-negotiated rates and streamlined billing, either charged directly to the employer or subsidised.
• Analysis of recent call records revealed a significant knowledge gap among patients, with 65% unclear about their specific coverage limitations.
• For instance, patients were largely unaware of their 5-session-per-year coverage and the distinction between services requiring co-pays versus those fully covered by their employer.
• Despite this confusion, there is currently no strategy in place to enhance communication and reduce this volume of calls.
• The lack of clarity in billing and coverage details has led to an increasing number of unnecessary service calls, impacting our overall efficiency.
• This communication gap not only strains our customer service resources but also risks patient satisfaction and retention.
• Our current communication strategy may need to consider incorporating AI, given its growing prominence in healthcare patient communication as suggested by industry leaders.
subsections.justification: This content details the existing issues within customer service interaction, which is crucial for strategizing improvements to reduce service calls and enhance patient communication.

subsections.heading: Patient Communication Improvement Strategies
subsections.content:
• We aim to provide ongoing staff training on effective communication and empathetic listening
• Implementing a clearer, more user-friendly interface on our platform
• Exploring opportunities for enhancing communication through email and text messaging
• Considering the use of AI to automate routine inquiries
• Introducing a feedback system for customers to voice their concerns and suggestions
• Developing a comprehensive FAQ section on our website to address common inquiries
• Regularly updating our online resources for customer education
subsections.justification: This slide discusses possible strategies for improving communication but doesn't specifically address how this could reduce service calls in Q12025.

subsections.heading: Anticipated Impact on Service Calls
subsections.content:
• A 10% reduction in service calls is expected with the implementation of a new FAQ section
• Anticipate an increase in call volume during the initial roll-out of new communication strategies
• A potential 15% decrease in calls related to appointment scheduling with a more user-friendly interface
• Expect a surge of inquiries following the launch of any new platform features
• A 5% reduction in calls routed to the medical team is anticipated with better staff training
• A significant reduction in waiting times is expected with the use of AI for routine inquiries
subsections.justification: This slide talks about the impact of different strategies on service calls, but it doesn't specifically relate to improving patient communication for reducing service calls into Q12025.

conclusion: By implementing these patient communication strategies, we can expect to see a significant decrease in service calls. Let's aim to roll out these initiatives by the start of Q1 2025 to assess their effectiveness.
